name: Bankruptcy
window_name: Bankruptcy of legal entities — law company MRZ LEGAL.
meta_description: Bankruptcy from the firms leading practice. With a special focus on protecting interests of business owners and their assets.

blocks.text_main:
We do bankruptcy trials for legal entities. Firm’s law practice for entrepreneurs and companies with business of any scale.

Business owners commonly have a desire for a return on investment in both successful and bankrupt situations. Nonetheless, this desire is valid in the former case and usually invalid in the latter.

This purpose becomes unlawful when a controlling person claims for the return of property that belongs to an entity, therefore granting them the status of an ordinary creditor in the bankruptcy case. This claim arises at a time when that person foresees bankruptcy in the future or tries to circumvent it in the present.

In this scenario, an arbitral tribunal determines that a beneficiary intended to capture the value of the legal entity’s assets if it goes bankrupt, thereby disregarding the priority rights of independent creditors by adding their own claim in the register of creditors’ claims.

When determining the participant’s behaviour of evading the filing for corporate bankruptcy by offering compensation financing, courts conclude that he intentionally selected this form of financing to hide the property crisis from creditors, which serves as the grounds for submitting a debtor’s petition.

Consequently, prior to commencement of the initial bankruptcy procedure, courts decline to acknowledge the claim presented by a controlling person and do not include it in the registry of creditors’ claims. Similarly, they do not deem transactions associated with the withdrawal of assets from the entity as invalid.

In general, current trends are largely unfavourable for business owners whose property interests are overlooked due to insufficient assets belonging to a legal entity. This lack of balance compromises the rights and interests of independent creditors.

We align the interests of beneficiaries and creditors manually, creating advantages for our clients on either side of bankruptcy cases.
